Curriculum Management
Organise all CBC learning areas, strands, and sub-strands. Assign teachers to subjects and track coverage across Pre-Primary to Senior Secondary.
Assessment & Grading
Create rubrics, record formative and summative assessments, and grade students on the 4-level competency scale (Exceeding, Meeting, Approaching, Below).
Progress Tracking
View each student\'s competency levels across all subjects with heatmaps and progress dashboards. Parents can see their child\'s standing in real time.
Evidence & Submissions
Students upload projects, documents, and videos as proof of mastery. Teachers review submissions and provide feedback directly on the platform.
For Teachers
Manage your classes, create assessments with rubrics, score students on competency levels, and generate term reports — all from one dashboard.
For Parents
See exactly where your child stands across every learning area. Track their competency levels, view teacher feedback, and monitor progress over time.
For School Admins
Oversee curriculum implementation across the entire school. Manage teacher assignments, view school-wide analytics, and generate compliance reports.

pages.title: About Us (Mission & Vision)
pages.content:
About NONEAA
Explore Features
Talk to Us
Who We Are
Building the Future of Education in Kenya
NONEAA was born from a simple observation: Kenya's Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) is revolutionary, but schools need better digital tools to implement it effectively. We bridge that gap.
Our Purpose
Mission & Vision
Guiding principles that drive everything we build
Our Mission
To empower every Kenyan school with intelligent digital tools that make competency-based education achievable, measurable, and impactful — ensuring no student is left behind.
Our Vision
A future where every African student accesses world-class, personalized education that recognizes and develops their unique competencies — regardless of location or background.
Our Growth
Growing Across Kenya
From 5 pilot schools in 2023 to 120+ schools transforming education today
Platform Adoption
Schools, students, and teachers over time
Regional Presence
Distribution of partner schools across Kenya
Proven Results
Measurable Student Improvement

The Problem
Schools across Kenya struggle with manual CBC assessment tracking, inconsistent reporting, and lack of data-driven insights — leaving teachers overwhelmed and students underserved.
Our Solution
A comprehensive digital platform that automates competency tracking, generates real-time analytics, and provides AI-powered recommendations — making CBC implementation seamless.
The Impact
Schools using NONEAA see 32% improvement in student competency scores, 4.5 hours saved per teacher weekly, and 98% teacher satisfaction with our assessment tools.
Real-time competency tracking across all 7 CBC competencies
AI-powered personalized learning recommendations
Teacher empowerment through actionable data insights
Seamless KICD curriculum alignment and reporting
Accessible and affordable for all school types
Equitable educational outcomes nationwide
Technology that amplifies teacher expertise
Pan-African expansion with local curriculum support
Schools Transformed
Students Impacted
Teachers Empowered
Satisfaction Rate
CBC-Native Design
Built from the ground up for Kenya\'s 2-6-3-3-3 education structure and KICD standards — not a foreign platform retrofitted.
7 Competency Tracking
Track all 7 CBC competencies (Communication, Critical Thinking, Creativity, Digital Literacy, Citizenship, Self-Efficacy, Learning to Learn) in real time.
AI-Powered Insights
Machine learning algorithms identify struggling students early, recommend interventions, and predict competency trajectories.
Works Offline
Designed for Kenyan realities — works on low-bandwidth connections and syncs data when internet is available.
KDPA Compliant
Full compliance with Kenya Data Protection Act. Student data is encrypted, anonymized, and stored on local servers.
Dedicated Support
Every school gets a dedicated onboarding specialist, training sessions, and 24/7 WhatsApp support from our team.
The Spark
Identified the gap between CBC curriculum goals and available digital tools. Started research with 10 teachers in Nairobi.
Pilot Launch
Deployed v1 in 5 pilot schools in Nairobi. Tracked 800 students across 40 classes, collecting critical feedback.
Product-Market Fit
Expanded to 15 schools after 95% teacher approval. Added strand-level assessment tracking and parent report cards.
Regional Expansion
Grew to 35 schools across Nairobi, Mombasa, and Kisumu. Launched mobile app for offline assessment capture.
AI Integration
Introduced AI-powered predictive analytics, identifying at-risk students 6 weeks before traditional methods. Reached 85 schools.
Nationwide Scale
Now serving 120+ schools, 19,200+ students, and 960+ teachers. Preparing for East Africa expansion into Uganda and Tanzania.
Innovation First
We challenge conventional edtech norms to build solutions that truly work in African classrooms.
Excellence Always
Every feature is tested with real teachers, iterated on real feedback, and held to the highest quality bar.
Community Driven
We co-create with educators, parents, and education officials — our roadmap is shaped by the people we serve.
Impact at Scale
Our success is measured not by revenue, but by the number of students whose lives are transformed.
NONEAA has completely changed how we track student competencies. Before, I spent 3 hours every week on manual records. Now it takes 20 minutes, and I can actually see which students need help.
Jane Wanjiku
Grade 4 Teacher
The parent report cards generated by NONEAA are incredible. Parents finally understand what CBC means for their children. Our parent engagement has gone up by 60% since we started using the platform.
David Ochieng
School Principal
As a county education officer, I can now see real-time data from 15 schools in my jurisdiction. The analytics dashboard helps me allocate resources where they are needed most.
Mary Kiptoo
County Education Officer

How long is the demo?
Most demos take 45–60 minutes, depending on the modules your team wants to focus on.
Is the demo free?
Yes. The demo is completely free with no obligation.
Can multiple staff members attend?
Absolutely. We encourage principals, ICT officers, bursars, and academic leads to join.
Do you offer implementation support?
Yes. We support onboarding, setup, training, and post-launch assistance.
How soon can we get started after the demo?
Most schools begin onboarding within 1–2 weeks depending on readiness and timelines.

Getting Started
How do I create my first classroom?
Navigate to the Classroom section in your dashboard, click 'Create New Classroom', and follow the guided setup wizard. You can add students, set up assignments, and customize settings.
How do I enroll students?
Go to the Students section, click 'Add Student', and fill in the required information. You can import multiple students via CSV or connect with your school's SIS.
What are the system requirements?
Noneaa works on any modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) with an internet connection. Mobile apps are available for iOS and Android.
Teaching & Learning
How do students submit assignments?
Students access their dashboard, navigate to the Assignments tab, and upload their work. You'll receive notifications when assignments are submitted.
Can I track student progress in real-time?
Yes, the Analytics dashboard provides real-time insights into student performance, engagement metrics, and learning progress.
How do I create assessments?
Use the Assessment Builder to create quizzes, tests, and exams. You can choose from multiple question types and set automatic grading.
Technical Support
What if I forget my password?
Click 'Forgot Password' on the login page. Enter your email address and follow the instructions to reset your password securely.
Is my data backed up?
Yes, we perform automatic daily backups and retain data for 30 days. Enterprise plans offer extended backup retention.
How do I integrate with other tools?
We offer API access and pre-built integrations with popular educational tools. Visit our Integrations page for more details.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.content:
Privacy Policy
Your privacy is important to us. This policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information when using the NONEAA platform.
Last updated: June 2026
Our Commitment
Privacy Principles We Live By
These principles guide every decision we make about your data.
Data Retention
We retain your personal data only as long as necessary to provide our services and comply with legal obligations.
Account data: retained while your account is active
Academic records: retained per school policy and regulations
Usage logs: retained for 12 months
Deleted data: permanently purged within 30 days
Cookies & Tracking
We use cookies to keep you logged in and improve your experience. We do not use advertising or tracking cookies.
Essential cookies: login session and security
Preference cookies: language and UI settings
Analytics: anonymized usage statistics only
No third-party advertising cookies — ever
As an educational platform, we take extra care with student data. All learner data is managed through school administrator and parent accounts. Students under 18 cannot create accounts independently.
Questions About Your Privacy?
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, our Data Protection Officer is here to help.
privacy@noneaa.com
www.noneaa.com/privacy
Contact Us
Transparency
We clearly explain what data we collect, why we collect it, and how it is used.
Minimal Collection
We only collect data that is strictly necessary for providing our educational services.
Strong Encryption
All data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256) — no exceptions.
User Control
You own your data. Export, correct, or delete your information at any time.
No Selling Data
We never sell, trade, or share personal data with third parties for marketing.
Compliance
Fully compliant with the Kenya Data Protection Act 2019 and GDPR standards.
Information We Collect
Personal Information
We may collect the following personal information when you register or use our platform:

pages.title: Terms of Service
pages.content:
Terms of Service
Last updated: June 2026
Have Questions About Our Terms?
legal@noneaa.com
www.noneaa.com/terms
Contact Us
Fair & Transparent
Our terms are written in plain language — no hidden clauses or legal tricks.
Education First
Every policy is designed to support schools, teachers, parents, and learners.
Data Ownership
Schools and users retain ownership of all educational data they create.
Flexible Plans
Upgrade, downgrade, or cancel your subscription at any time without penalty.
1. Acceptance of Terms
2. Description of Service
3. User Accounts
Account Creation
To access certain features, you must create an account through your school administrator. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and all activities under your account.
Account Responsibilities
You agree to provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and keep it updated. Notify us immediately of any unauthorized access to your account.
Account Termination
We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms. Schools may request account closure at any time, and we will export data before deletion upon request.
4. Acceptable Use Policy
5. Intellectual Property
6. Payment & Subscription
Billing
Paid plans are billed on a per-term or annual basis. Prices are displayed in KES and are inclusive of applicable taxes unless stated otherwise.
Refunds
We offer a 14-day refund window from the start of a new subscription. After 14 days, the current billing period is non-refundable, but you may cancel to prevent future charges.
Free Tier
We offer a free tier with limited features. Schools can upgrade at any time. No credit card is required for the free tier.
7. Limitation of Liability
8. Governing Law
9. Changes to Terms

pages.title: Security
pages.content:
Security at NONEAA
Last updated: June 2026
Defense in Depth
Multiple Layers of Protection
Your data is protected by six interconnected security layers — each one designed to prevent, detect, and respond to threats.
Architecture
How We Protect Your Data
Each layer adds protection — an attacker would need to bypass all five to reach your data.
Best Practices
Security Beyond Technology
Security is about people and processes too — not just technology.
Compliance & Certifications
Report a Vulnerability
Found a security issue? We take all reports seriously and respond within 48 hours. Responsible disclosure is rewarded.
security@noneaa.com
Security Questions?
Need more details about our security practices? Our security team is happy to answer questions and provide documentation.
Contact Security Team
Uptime SLA
Data Breaches
Monitoring
Encryption
Data Encryption
Access Controls
Infrastructure Security
Monitoring & Detection
Incident Response
Compliance & Audits
Secure Development
All code goes through security-focused code reviews. We follow OWASP Top 10 guidelines and conduct static analysis on every release.
Employee Security
All team members undergo background checks and security training. Access to production systems is limited to essential personnel only.
Backup & Recovery
Automated daily backups with 30-day retention. Recovery tested quarterly. RPO < 1 hour, RTO < 4 hours.
Responsible Disclosure
We welcome security researchers to report vulnerabilities through our responsible disclosure program. We respond within 48 hours.
DDoS protection, WAF, rate limiting, SSL/TLS termination
Authentication, RBAC, input validation, CSRF protection
AES-256 encryption, field-level encryption, data masking
Network segmentation, container isolation, secrets management
Audit logs, anomaly detection, real-time alerting, SIEM
Kenya DPA 2019
Data Protection Act compliant
GDPR Aligned
EU data protection standards
SOC 2 Type II
Security & availability certified
ISO 27001

pages.title: CBC Standards
pages.content:
CBE Standards & Framework
Understanding Kenya's Competency-Based Curriculum (CBE) and our commitment to educational excellence.
Last updated: January 2026
What is the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBE)?
The Competency-Based Curriculum (CBE) is Kenya's educational framework designed to nurture every learner's potential through a holistic approach to education. Introduced in 2017, CBE focuses on developing competencies, skills, and values rather than just theoretical knowledge.
Our platform aligns with the Kenya Institute of Curriculum Development (KICD) standards, ensuring that educational institutions can effectively implement and track CBE learning outcomes.
Core Competencies
CBE focuses on seven core competencies that learners should develop:
Learning Areas
CBE organizes learning into the following areas:
Assessment and Evaluation
Formative Assessment
Ongoing assessment conducted during the learning process to monitor learner progress and provide feedback for improvement. Our platform supports continuous formative assessment through various tools and tracking mechanisms.
Summative Assessment
Conducted at the end of a learning period to evaluate learner achievement against set standards. The platform facilitates comprehensive summative assessments aligned with KICD guidelines.
Competency-Based Assessment
Assessment focuses on what learners can do with their knowledge and skills, rather than just what they know. Our system tracks competency development across all learning areas.
CBE Values and Principles
The CBE is founded on these core values:
Respect:
For self, others, and the environment
Unity:
Promoting national cohesion and patriotism
Responsibility:
Being accountable for one's actions
Peace:
Fostering peaceful coexistence and conflict resolution
Love:
Caring for others and showing compassion
Integrity:
Honesty and adherence to moral principles
Patriotism:
Love and devotion to one's country
How Our Platform Supports CBE
Noneaa is specifically designed to support CBE implementation:
CBE Education Levels
Early Years Education (EYE)
Pre-Primary 1 and Pre-Primary 2 (ages 4-5 years) - Focus on play-based learning and early childhood development.
Lower Primary
Grades 1-3 - Building foundational literacy, numeracy, and basic competencies.
Upper Primary
Grades 4-6 - Deepening knowledge and skills across learning areas.
Lower Secondary
Grades 7-9 - Introducing specialized learning pathways and career exploration.
Senior Secondary
Grades 10-12 - Focused pathway learning in preparation for tertiary education or career pathways.
Additional Resources
Learn more about Kenya's CBE framework
For more information about the Competency-Based Curriculum, visit:
Kenya Institute of Curriculum Development (KICD) -
kicd.ac.ke
Ministry of Education - Official CBE Guidelines and Resources
Teachers Service Commission (TSC) - CBE Implementation Support
Questions About CBE Standards?
Our team is here to help you understand and implement CBE effectively
Email
cbe-support@noneaa.africa
Phone
Address
Noneaa Africa
Nairobi, Kenya
CBE Curriculum Team
curriculum@noneaa.africa
Communication and Collaboration
Ability to listen, speak, read, and write in various modes and contexts. Working effectively with others in diverse settings.
Critical Thinking and Problem Solving
Analyzing issues, making informed decisions, and finding creative solutions to challenges.
Imagination and Creativity
Using imagination to generate new ideas, products, and solutions through innovation and inventiveness.
Citizenship
Understanding rights, responsibilities, and duties as a Kenyan citizen and global citizen.
Digital Literacy
Effectively using digital technologies to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information.
Learning to Learn
Taking charge of one\'s own learning and pursuing knowledge independently and collaboratively.
Self-Efficacy
Developing confidence in one\'s abilities to achieve goals and overcome challenges.
Competency Tracking
Track learner progress across all seven core competencies with detailed analytics.
Curriculum Alignment
All content and assessments are mapped to KICD curriculum designs and learning outcomes.
Formative Assessment Tools
Built-in tools for continuous assessment and feedback aligned with CBE principles.
Progress Reports
Comprehensive reports that communicate learner development to parents and guardians.
Teacher Support
Resources and tools to help teachers effectively deliver CBE lessons and track outcomes.
Data-Driven Insights
Analytics dashboard to monitor school-wide CBE implementation and learner performance.

Experiencing issues with the platform? Our technical team can help troubleshoot and resolve problems quickly.
Partnership Inquiries
Interested in collaborating? We work with NGOs, government bodies, and education partners across East Africa.
General Inquiries
Have questions about our pricing, features, or how NONEAA works? We're happy to help.
How does NONEAA help with CBE assessment?
NONEAA provides comprehensive CBE assessment tools that track learner competencies across all 7 CBC areas, generate detailed reports, and help teachers identify where students need additional support. Our platform aligns fully with Kenya's KICD curriculum framework.
Is NONEAA available on mobile devices?
Yes. NONEAA is fully responsive and works on mobile devices, tablets, and desktops. It also works offline and syncs when internet is available — designed for Kenyan school realities.
How much does NONEAA cost?
We offer flexible pricing plans tailored to schools of all sizes — from single-stream primary schools to large multi-campus institutions. Contact our sales team for a custom quote based on your student count and feature needs.
Do you provide training for teachers?
Yes. Every onboarding includes comprehensive training for administrators and teachers. We offer both in-person workshops and virtual training sessions, plus ongoing support via WhatsApp, documentation, and video guides.
Is my school data secure?
Absolutely. We use enterprise-grade encryption, comply with Kenya's Data Protection Act (KDPA), and maintain SOC 2 aligned practices. All data is stored in secure cloud infrastructure with regular backups and strict access controls.
How long does it take to set up NONEAA for my school?
Most schools are fully onboarded within 1–2 weeks. This includes data migration, staff training, and curriculum configuration. Larger institutions with complex requirements may take 3–4 weeks.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.content:
Getting Started
Set Up Your School in Under an Hour
Follow these 5 steps to get Noneaa fully configured for your school. Most institutions complete setup in 30–60 minutes. Our team is available to help at every step.
Need Help Along the Way?
Our support team and resources are available whenever you need them.
Ready to Get Started?
Create your school account today. Our onboarding team will reach out within 24 hours to help you through the setup process.
Create School Account
Create Your School Account
Sign up with your school email and verify your institution. You\'ll get immediate access to set up your school profile, including school name, code, level (Primary, Junior Secondary, Senior Secondary), and term dates.
Add Your Team
Invite teachers and administrators to the platform. You can add them manually or import an entire staff list from a CSV file. Each user gets role-based access — teachers see their classes, admins see everything.
Set Up Classes & Students
Create your class structure and add students. Import student data in bulk or add them one by one. Assign students to classes and set up subject/learning area allocations for each teacher.
Configure Assessments
Set up your assessment framework aligned with the CBC curriculum. Define strands, sub-strands, and competency indicators for each learning area. Use our pre-built templates or customize your own rubrics.
Start Tracking & Reporting
You\'re ready to go! Teachers can begin recording assessments, and the platform automatically tracks competency development. Generate beautiful term reports with one click at the end of each term.
Video Tutorials
Step-by-step video guides covering every feature of the platform.
Help Documentation
Detailed articles and how-to guides for common tasks.
Live Training Sessions
Book a free training session for your teaching staff.

pages.title: CBE Methodology
pages.content:
CBE Methodology
The Future of Education
is Competency-Based
Get Started
View CBE Standards
Understanding CBE
What is Competency-Based Education?
CBE shifts the focus from what learners
know
to what they can
. Instead of cramming for exams, learners develop practical skills, critical thinking abilities, and values that prepare them for real life.
Kenya adopted the CBC structure (2-6-3-3-3) to replace the traditional 8-4-4 system. Our platform is built from the ground up to support this new approach.
Core Competencies
The 7 Pillars of CBE
Every learner is assessed across these 7 competencies, ensuring holistic development beyond academic subjects.
Assessment Framework
Four Levels of Competency
The Journey
CBC Implementation Timeline
Our Platform
How NONEAA Brings CBE to Life
Embrace the CBE Methodology Today
Join the movement toward competency-based education. NONEAA makes the transition seamless for your school.
Get Started Free
Request Demo
Communication & Collaboration
Developing effective listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills. Working productively with others in diverse settings.
Critical Thinking & Problem Solving
Analyzing issues, evaluating evidence, and making reasoned decisions. Finding creative solutions to real-world problems.
Creativity & Imagination
Using imagination and inventiveness to generate new ideas, products, and approaches to everyday challenges.
Citizenship
Understanding rights and responsibilities. Developing respect for diversity, democratic values, and national identity.
Digital Literacy
Navigating, evaluating, and creating information using digital technologies safely, ethically, and effectively.
Learning to Learn
Developing self-awareness, self-regulation, and the ability to manage one\'s own learning journey.
Self-Efficacy
Building confidence in one\'s ability to succeed. Developing resilience, perseverance, and a growth mindset.
Exceeding Expectation
Learner demonstrates understanding and application beyond the expected competency level.
Meeting Expectation
Learner demonstrates the expected competency level for their grade.
Approaching Expectation
Learner is progressing toward the expected competency level.
Below Expectation
Learner needs additional support to achieve the expected competency level.
Kenya Institute of Curriculum Development introduces the Competency-Based Curriculum to replace 8-4-4 system.
Pre-primary levels begin implementing CBC nationwide across all public and private schools.
CBC extends to upper primary. Assessment moves from exams to continuous competency evaluation.
Grade 7-9 (Junior Secondary) starts under the new 2-6-3-3-3 education structure.
Digital platforms like NONEAA enable schools to fully manage CBC assessments, reporting, and competency tracking.
Old System
New System
Assessment
Competencies

Learner Portfolios

Data-Driven Decisions
School leaders get dashboards showing school-wide competency trends and areas needing attention.
